hypothesis: If a Transformer-based encoder–decoder model uses patch-based (150 cm⁻¹) spectrum embedding, is pretrained on simulated molecular-dynamics IR spectra and fine-tuned on NIST data, and is jointly trained with a spectral reconstruction alignment loss (L = L_S2S + λL_reconstruction), then it will more accurately identify molecular components from binary and ternary mixture IR spectra—including experimental ATR‑IR measurements—across concentration ratios than an otherwise identical model lacking patch embedding, pretraining, or the reconstruction loss.
